result.summary: New Relic exposes a GraphQL API (NerdGraph), multiple HTTP APIs (REST v2 plus ingest APIs for events, metrics, logs, traces, security data, and blob storage), and an MCP server; all documented surfaces require New Relic-issued credentials.
result.description: New Relic is an observability platform for collecting, querying, and acting on telemetry such as metrics, logs, traces, and security findings. It also provides configuration and account-management capabilities for monitored services and infrastructure.

nr_user_key.label: New Relic User API key
nr_user_key.setup: In [New Relic API keys](https://one.newrelic.com/api-keys) or the [API keys UI launcher](https://one.newrelic.com/launcher/api-keys-ui.api-keys-launcher), create a **User** key from the user menu (**API keys**). This key is used for querying/configuration APIs such as [NerdGraph](https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/apis/nerdgraph/get-started/introduction-new-relic-nerdgraph/), the [REST API v2](https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/apis/rest-api-v2/get-started/introduction-new-relic-rest-api-v2/), and the [Blob Storage API](https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/apis/intro-apis/blob-storage-api/).

nr_mcp_oauth.label: New Relic MCP OAuth app access
nr_mcp_oauth.setup: Enable the preview in New Relic UI (**Administration > Previews & Trials**) as described in [MCP overview](https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/agentic-ai/mcp/overview/), then configure your MCP client with the New Relic OAuth settings from [Set up New Relic MCP](https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/agentic-ai/mcp/setup/): client ID `pUWGgnjsQ0bydqCbavTPpw==`, authorization URL `https://login.newrelic.com/login`, token URL `https://mcp.newrelic.com/oauth2/token` (EU: `https://mcp.eu.newrelic.com/oauth2/token`), scope `openid`. Complete the browser OAuth flow from your MCP client.

variables.name: region
variables.description: Use `https://api.eu.newrelic.com/graphql` for EU data center accounts.

variables.name: accountId
variables.description: New Relic account ID to receive the events.

variables.name: orgId
variables.description: New Relic organization ID appears in paths like `/organizations/{orgId}/AgentConfigurations`.
